backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor defining the backend for a team task manager, including types for Tasks, Users, Roles, and Activity entries, plus CRUD operations for tasks, user registration/login, password reset, team members, and activity logging with role-based access control.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report listing two features: ensuring the 'Add Team Member' button is visible and functional on the TeamMembers page, and auditing the AdminDashboard team members tab for the same action, each mapped to specific files and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: A JSON document containing short summaries of all frontend source files, describing their purpose, key changes, and notable implementation details for build planning reference.
frontend/index.css: Base Tailwind CSS file defining OKLCH CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens including background, foreground, primary, sidebar, chart, and destructive colors.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point for the React app titled 'Team Task Manager', mounting the app into a root div and loading the main TSX module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React app component that sets up TanStack Router with routes for login, signup, forgot-password, dashboard, tasks, activity log, team members, and my-tasks, wrapped in QueryClientProvider and AuthProvider.
frontend/src/components/AddUserModal.tsx: Modal dialog component for adding a new team member with fields for name, email, password, confirm password, and role, including validation and calling the useAddUser mutation on submit.
A modal dialog form for creating new tasks with fields for title, conference name, description, assignee, deadline, and priority, using validation and the useCreateTask mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/EditTaskModal.ts: A modal dialog form for editing an existing task, pre-populating fields from the task prop and submitting updates via the useEditTask mutation hook.
frontend/src/components/GoogleSignInButt: A sign-in button that authenticates users via Internet Identity, then fetches or creates their profile and redirects to the dashboard.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: The main app shell providing a responsive sidebar with navigation links, theme switcher, notification bell, user avatar, and logout functionality.
An empty file with no content.
frontend/src/components/NotificationBell: A bell icon button that displays a dropdown panel showing overdue/upcoming task reminders and recent login events, with an unread count badge.
frontend/src/components/PriorityBadge.ts: A small presentational component that renders a colored badge with an emoji and label for High, Medium, or Low task priority.
frontend/src/components/ReportExportButt: Renders export buttons (Weekly, Monthly, Custom Range) that filter tasks and download them as CSV files, with a popover for custom date range selection and validation.
frontend/src/components/TaskCard.tsx: Displays a single task's details (title, priority, urgency, deadline, assignee) with collapsible description, status control, and edit/delete actions.
frontend/src/components/TaskFilters.tsx: Provides a search input and assignee dropdown to filter tasks, with a clear button that appears when any filter is active.
frontend/src/components/TaskStatusContro: A dropdown select component for updating a task's status (Pending, In Progress, Completed, Carry Forward) with loading state and toast notifications.
frontend/src/components/TeamMemberCard.t: Expandable card showing a team member's task summary by status, fetching their tasks and rendering sorted TaskCard components when expanded.
frontend/src/components/ThemeSwitcher.ts: A segmented button group for switching between light, normal, and dark app themes using the useTheme hook, with tooltips for each option.
frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx: Provides authentication context with current user state, login/logout functions, and admin role detection, persisting the user to localStorage.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an Internet Computer actor using the current identity, initializing access control with an admin token and invalidating dependent queries when the actor changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: Re-exports the useAuth hook from the AuthContext for convenient importing.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication on the ICP platform, managing login, logout, and identity persistence via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Defines React Query hooks for all backend actor interactions including CRUD operations for tasks, team members, users, and activity logs.
frontend/src/hooks/useTaskFilters.ts: Custom hook that filters a list of tasks by search text and assignee using memoization.
frontend/src/hooks/useTaskReminders.ts: Custom hook that categorizes tasks into overdue, due-in-one-hour, due-in-24-hours, and carry-forward buckets with a total reminder count.
frontend/src/hooks/useTheme.ts: Custom hook that manages the app's dark/light/normal theme by persisting the selection to localStorage and applying CSS classes to the document root.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that defines CSS custom property tokens for dark, light, and normal themes using Tailwind's base/components/utilities layers.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/ActivityLog.tsx: Page component that displays a filterable, sorted activity log of task and login events with color-coded action badges.
frontend/src/pages/AdminDashboard.tsx: Admin dashboard page showing task stats, a filterable/sortable task list, and modals for creating, editing, deleting tasks, and adding users.
frontend/src/pages/ForgotPassword.tsx: Renders a forgot-password page where users enter their email to receive reset instructions, showing a confirmation screen after submission (currently simulated with a delay, no real API call).
frontend/src/pages/Login.tsx: Renders the login page with email/password fields, calls the login mutation, stores auth state via AuthContext, and redirects authenticated users to the home route.
frontend/src/pages/Signup.tsx: Renders the registration page, validates inputs, calls the register mutation followed by an automatic login mutation, then stores auth state and navigates to home.
frontend/src/pages/Tasks.tsx: Displays a filterable, sortable list of all tasks with create/edit/delete actions, export buttons, and skeleton loading states; admin-only delete is gated via AuthContext.
frontend/src/pages/TeamMembers.tsx: React page displaying team members with task stats per member, supporting admin-only add/remove actions via AddUserModal and useRemoveTeamMember hook.
frontend/src/pages/TeamMemberView.tsx: Shows tasks assigned to a selected team member (defaulting to the current user) along with summary stats for pending, in-progress, completed, and overdue tasks.
frontend/src/utils/csvExport.ts: Utility functions for generating, filtering, and downloading task data as CSV files, with support for weekly, monthly, and custom date range filters.
frontend/src/utils/reminderLogic.ts: Provides helper functions to check if a task is overdue, due within one hour, or due within twenty-four hours based on its deadline.
frontend/src/utils/taskSorting.ts: Exports a single function that sorts tasks by deadline ascending, then by priority (High > Medium > Low) as a tiebreaker.
frontend/src/utils/taskUrgency.ts: Defines urgency levels for tasks and provides functions to compute urgency, retrieve corresponding CSS classes, badge styles, and labels.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading URL query parameters (including hash-based routing), persisting them in sessionStorage, and cleaning sensitive params from the URL.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the theme with OKLCH-based design tokens for colors, custom fonts, border radii, shadows, and urgency-level color variables.
project_state.json: Project state metadata describing a fix request to make the 'Add Team Member' button visible and functional on the TeamMembers and AdminDashboard pages.
